title: Supported Distributions
text: Parameter names built accumulator.parameter. example, go.m, stop.shape, choice.v refer parameters accumulators go, stop, choice.

title: Supported Distributions
text: lognormal: m s, usual log-scale location spread parameters. gamma: shape rate. exgauss: mu, sigma, tau. LBA: v, B, , sv. RDM: v, B, , s. Outside accumulators can non-decision time t0 trigger probability q. exgauss t0 mu redundant (constitute shift rt) one must fixed.
code: data.frame(   distribution = c("lognormal", "gamma", "exgauss", "LBA", "RDM"),   parameters = c(     "m, s",     "shape, rate",     "mu, sigma, tau",     "v, B, A, sv",     "v, B, A, s"   ),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) ##   distribution     parameters ## 1    lognormal           m, s ## 2        gamma    shape, rate ## 3      exgauss mu, sigma, tau ## 4          LBA    v, B, A, sv ## 5          RDM     v, B, A, s

title: Supported Distributions
text: model can combine different accumulator distributions. thing changes parameter suffix used parameter vector. Choose distribution matches accumulator want specify, use parameter names consistently build_param_matrix(), simulate(), likelihood evaluation.
code: model <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "exgauss") |>   add_outcome("go", "go") |>   add_outcome("stop", "stop") |>   finalize_model()  params <- c(   go.m = log(0.30),   go.s = 0.18,   stop.mu = 0.10,   stop.sigma = 0.04,   stop.tau = 0.08 )  build_param_matrix(model, params, n_trials = 2) ##      q w t0        p1   p2   p3 ## [1,] 0 1  0 -1.203973 0.18 0.00 ## [2,] 0 1  0  0.100000 0.04 0.08 ## [3,] 0 1  0 -1.203973 0.18 0.00 ## [4,] 0 1  0  0.100000 0.04 0.08

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: workflows AccumulatR follow four steps: Define model accumulators, pools, observed responses. Finalize model finalize_model(). Work behavioral data, either simulated simulate() collected experiment. Prepare data, build model context, evaluate candidate parameter values.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: trial single behavioral observation. simplest case, row data corresponds one trial.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: R observed response label trial. two-choice task, typical values might "left" "right", "red" "green", etc.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: rt observed response time R.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: component serves two purposes. can used account different trial types data, example subset data requires different accumulator structures. can also used allow latent mixtures within trial. model one trial type, usually need column. See also Working Mixtures vignette.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: onset time accumulator becomes active. accumulators start time 0, cases, extra information appears trial. AccumulatR, onset can : fixed delay, onset = 0.15 chained onset, onset = (""), meaning process starts another accumulator (case ) pool finished onset non-decision time (t0) shift response times. difference AccumulatR t0 estimated parameter, whereas onset part model structure.

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: example defines basic two-choice race model two lognormal accumulators.
code: model <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("left", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("right", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("left", "left") |>   add_outcome("right", "right") |>   finalize_model()

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: can generate small behavioral dataset model. build_param_matrix makes one row parameters per trial. allows trial-wise manipulate specific parameters, example using design matrix specification. row one trial, R observed response, rt response time.
code: pars <- c(   left.m = log(0.28), left.s = 0.16,   right.m = log(0.35), right.s = 0.18 )  param_df <- build_param_matrix(model, pars, n_trials = 6) sim <- simulate(model, param_df, seed = 123)  head(sim) ##   trial     R        rt ## 1     1  left 0.3182631 ## 2     2  left 0.3414185 ## 3     3  left 0.2883234 ## 4     4  left 0.3669476 ## 5     5  left 0.3057125 ## 6     6 right 0.2456584

title: Getting Started with AccumulatR
text: fit model, usual pattern : prepare_data() reshapes behavioral data likelihood evaluation, make_context() builds model-side runtime state. log_likelihood() returns fit parameter set observed data.
code: prepared <- prepare_data(model, sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")]) ctx <- make_context(model) log_likelihood(ctx, prepared, param_df) ## [1] 5.291506

title: Logical Rules
text: outcome rule states observed response count happened. Common cases include: one several routes able generate several processes complete absence competing event stop-like process can cancel otherwise available response logical helpers encode cases directly model definition.

title: Logical Rules
text: main helpers : first_of(x, y) means response can generated either x y all_of(x, y) means response requires x y none_of(x) means response rule requires x happen inhibit(x, y) means x generate response, y can block

what: first_of(): either route can generate the response
title: Logical Rules
text: Use first_of() one several routes can generate response.
code: model_first <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("word_route", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("picture_route", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("target_detected", first_of("word_route", "picture_route")) |>   finalize_model()  model_first$outcomes[[1]]$label ## NULL

what: all_of(): several things must all be true
title: Logical Rules
text: Use all_of() response depends several processes complete.
code: model_all <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("encoding", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("verification", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("confirmed_response", all_of("encoding", "verification")) |>   finalize_model()  model_all$outcomes[[1]]$label ## NULL

what: none_of(): the response requires the competing event to be absent
title: Logical Rules
text: Use none_of() rule requires event happen. absence stop part response definition.
code: model_none <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("respond", all_of("go", none_of("stop"))) |>   finalize_model()  model_none$outcomes[[1]]$label ## NULL

what: inhibit(): the response is driven by one process and blocked by another
title: Logical Rules
text: Use inhibit() one process generate response another can block . go response-generating process stop blocks .
code: model_inhibit <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("respond", inhibit("go", "stop")) |>   finalize_model()  model_inhibit$outcomes[[1]]$label ## NULL

title: Logical Rules
text: two rules look similar: model statement.
code: rule_none <- all_of("go", none_of("stop")) rule_inhibit <- inhibit("go", "stop")  rule_none ## $kind ## [1] "and" ##  ## $args ## $args[[1]] ## $args[[1]]$kind ## [1] "event" ##  ## $args[[1]]$source ## [1] "go" ##  ## $args[[1]]$k ## NULL ##  ##  ## $args[[2]] ## $args[[2]]$kind ## [1] "not" ##  ## $args[[2]]$arg ## $args[[2]]$arg$kind ## [1] "event" ##  ## $args[[2]]$arg$source ## [1] "stop" ##  ## $args[[2]]$arg$k ## NULL rule_inhibit ## $kind ## [1] "guard" ##  ## $blocker ## $blocker$kind ## [1] "event" ##  ## $blocker$source ## [1] "stop" ##  ## $blocker$k ## NULL ##  ##  ## $reference ## $reference$kind ## [1] "event" ##  ## $reference$source ## [1] "go" ##  ## $reference$k ## NULL

title: Logical Rules
text: defines response terms two conditions: go happened stop happen.

title: Logical Rules
text: defines go response-generating event stop blocker event. short: inhibit() means “stop can block go gets time” none_of() means “response requires stop occur”

title: Logical Rules
text: helpers can combined response depends several kinds logic . example, suppose response requires: encoding stage finish either left route right route generate choice stop event Use combined rules observed response reflects structured decision rule rather single winning accumulator.
code: model_combo <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("encoding", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("left_route", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("right_route", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome(     "choice",     all_of(       "encoding",       first_of("left_route", "right_route"),       none_of("stop")     )   ) |>   finalize_model()  model_combo$outcomes[[1]]$label ## NULL

title: Logical Rules
text: practical guide: use first_of() several routes can produce response use all_of() several conditions must satisfied use none_of() response rule explicitly requires event absent use inhibit() one process produce response another process can cancel none_of() treats absence part response definition. inhibit() treats one process blocking another.

title: Working with Mixtures
text: sections use fast-versus-slow architecture. Response R1 comes target route response R2 comes competitor. components differ whether target route fast slow given trial.

what: Fixed mixture weights with observed components
title: Working with Mixtures
text: first example, mixture weights fixed component label observed trial. now create trial table says component trial belongs . component known , simulated data keep label. pattern applies real data: trial type observed, keep component column.
code: model_fixed <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("target_fast", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("target_slow", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("competitor", "lognormal") |>   add_pool("TARGET", c("target_fast", "target_slow")) |>   add_outcome("R1", "TARGET") |>   add_outcome("R2", "competitor") |>   add_component("fast", members = c("target_fast", "competitor"), weight = 0.4) |>   add_component("slow", members = c("target_slow", "competitor"), weight = 0.6) |>   set_mixture_options(mode = "fixed") |>   finalize_model()  true_params_fixed <- c(   target_fast.m = log(0.25),   target_fast.s = 0.15,   target_slow.m = log(0.45),   target_slow.s = 0.20,   competitor.m = log(0.35),   competitor.s = 0.18 ) set.seed(123456)  n_trials_fixed <- 1200 trial_types <- data.frame(   trial = seq_len(n_trials_fixed),   component = sample(c("fast", "slow"), n_trials_fixed, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.6)),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  params_df_fixed <- build_param_matrix(   model_fixed,   true_params_fixed,   trial_df = trial_types )  sim_fixed <- simulate(   model_fixed,   params_df_fixed,   trial_df = trial_types,   layout = "long" )  data_fixed <- sim_fixed[, c("trial", "R", "rt", "component")]  table(data_fixed$component, data_fixed$R) ##        ##         R1  R2 ##   fast 452  30 ##   slow 131 587 prepared_fixed <- prepare_data(model_fixed, data_fixed) ctx_fixed <- make_context(model_fixed)  params_df_fixed_true <- build_param_matrix(   model_fixed,   true_params_fixed,   trial_df = prepared_fixed )  ll_fixed <- as.numeric(log_likelihood(ctx_fixed, prepared_fixed, params_df_fixed_true)) ll_fixed ## [1] 1554.949

what: Sampled mixture weights with latent components
title: Working with Mixtures
text: Now use architecture latent mixture. component observed per trial. Instead, model samples hidden component trial, p_fast controlling proportion fast trials. default, sampled mixtures expose component label output, label latent. want inspect hidden generating component, can ask explicitly. likelihood evaluation, keep sampled-mixture data form real latent-mixture data: observed response R response time rt. component omitted , likelihood marginalizes possible components. supply component column, component treated observed trials.
code: model_sampled <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("target_fast", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("target_slow", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("competitor", "lognormal") |>   add_pool("TARGET", c("target_fast", "target_slow")) |>   add_outcome("R1", "TARGET") |>   add_outcome("R2", "competitor") |>   add_component("fast", members = c("target_fast", "competitor"), weight_param = "p_fast") |>   add_component("slow", members = c("target_slow", "competitor")) |>   set_mixture_options(mode = "sample", reference = "slow") |>   finalize_model()  true_params_sampled <- c(   target_fast.m = log(0.25),   target_fast.s = 0.15,   target_slow.m = log(0.45),   target_slow.s = 0.20,   competitor.m = log(0.35),   competitor.s = 0.18,   p_fast = 0.35 ) set.seed(123456)  n_trials_sampled <- 1500 params_df_sampled <- build_param_matrix(   model_sampled,   true_params_sampled,   n_trials = n_trials_sampled )  sim_sampled <- simulate(model_sampled, params_df_sampled) names(sim_sampled) ## [1] "trial" "R"     "rt" sim_sampled_with_component <- simulate(   model_sampled,   params_df_sampled,   keep_component = TRUE )  table(sim_sampled_with_component$component) ##  ## fast slow  ##  541  959 data_sampled <- sim_sampled[, c("trial", "R", "rt")]  prepared_sampled <- prepare_data(model_sampled, data_sampled) ctx_sampled <- make_context(model_sampled)  params_df_sampled_true <- build_param_matrix(   model_sampled,   true_params_sampled,   trial_df = prepared_sampled )  ll_sampled_true <- as.numeric(log_likelihood(ctx_sampled, prepared_sampled, params_df_sampled_true)) ll_sampled_true ## [1] 1169.475

title: Working with Mixtures
text: Latent weights can also estimated. keep example simple, estimate latent mixture weight p_fast, holding accumulator timing parameters fixed. Use mode = "fixed" mixture weights known mode = "sample" mixture weights estimated. Independently choice, include component column component observed trial. Otherwise omit , set NA, likelihood marginalizes components.
code: neg_loglik <- function(theta) {   est <- true_params_sampled   est["p_fast"] <- plogis(theta[["logit_p_fast"]])   params_df <- build_param_matrix(     model_sampled,     est,     trial_df = prepared_sampled   )   ll <- log_likelihood(ctx_sampled, prepared_sampled, params_df)   -as.numeric(ll) }  start <- c(logit_p_fast = qlogis(0.60))  set.seed(123456) fit <- optim(start, neg_loglik, method = "BFGS") fit_params <- c(p_fast = plogis(fit$par[["logit_p_fast"]])) target <- c(p_fast = true_params_sampled[["p_fast"]])  data.frame(true = target, recovered = fit_params, miss = abs(target - fit_params)) ##        true recovered       miss ## p_fast 0.35 0.3702799 0.02027991

what: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: AccumulatR R/C++ toolkit race-model simulation likelihood evaluation. built kinds models cognitive psychologists use responses generated competing evidence-accumulation processes. can define model, simulate behavioral data, evaluate well different parameter values explain observed response times choices.

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: AccumulatR yet CRAN. install current development version GitHub:
code: remotes::install_github("niekstevenson/AccumulatR")

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: AccumulatR organized around simple workflow: Define model accumulators, pools, observed responses. Turn specification object ready simulation fitting. Simulate behavioral data known parameter values. Evaluate log-likelihood observed data candidate parameters.

title: Simulation and Likelihood Tools for Evidence-Accumulation Models
text: example defines two-choice race model, simulates response-time data, evaluates likelihood data generating parameters. simulate() returns behavioral data one row per trial. simplest case means response column (R) response-time column (rt). log_likelihood() evaluates probable data set model parameters. longer worked examples, see package articles pkgdown site.
code: library(AccumulatR)  spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("left", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("right", "lognormal") |>   add_outcome("left", "left") |>   add_outcome("right", "right")  model <- finalize_model(spec)  pars <- c(   left.m = log(0.28), left.s = 0.16, left.q = 0, left.t0 = 0,   right.m = log(0.35), right.s = 0.18, right.q = 0, right.t0 = 0 )  param_df <- build_param_matrix(spec, pars, n_trials = 8) sim <- simulate(model, param_df, seed = 123) head(sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")])  prepared <- prepare_data(model, sim[c("trial", "R", "rt")]) ctx <- make_context(model) log_likelihood(ctx, prepared, param_df)

title: AccumulatR: Simulate and fit evidence-accumulation models — AccumulatR-package
text: `AccumulatR` researchers work response-time models behavior generated competing interacting accumulators. package lets describe model psychologically meaningful terms, simulate behavioral data, evaluate likelihoods fitting. main workflow : 1. Start `race_spec()`. 2. Add accumulators, pools, outcomes, timing relations. 3. Call `finalize_model()` prepare model simulation fitting. 4. Use `simulate()`, `prepare_data()`, `make_context()`, `log_likelihood()`. R interface intended stay readable model-focused, repeated numerical work happens C++.

title: AccumulatR: Simulate and fit evidence-accumulation models — AccumulatR-package
text: Maintainer: Niek Stevenson niek.stevenson@gmail.com

$accumulators[[1]]$id #> [1] "A" #>  #> $accumulators[[1]]$dist #> [1] "lognormal" #>  #> $accumulators[[1]]$onset #> [1] 0 #>  #>  #>  #> $pools #> list() #>  #> $outcomes #> $outcomes[[1]] #> what: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
text: Compute log-likelihood prepared behavioral data one candidate parameter sets.

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
code: log_likelihood(   context,   data,   parameters,   ok = NULL,   expand = NULL,   min_ll = log(1e-10),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'accumulatr_context' log_likelihood(   context,   data,   parameters,   ok = NULL,   expand = NULL,   min_ll = log(1e-10),   ... )  # Default S3 method log_likelihood(context, ...)

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
text: context Context created `make_context()`. data Prepared data created `prepare_data()`. parameters parameter data frame, list parameter data frames. ok Logical vector marking trials contribute likelihood. Trials marked `FALSE` assigned `min_ll`. expand Optional index vector used expand compressed trial-level results back original trial count. min_ll Minimum log-likelihood value used excluded impossible trials. ... Unused; S3 compatibility.

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
text: numeric vector log-likelihood values.

title: Evaluate the log-likelihood of behavioral data — log_likelihood
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params_df <- build_param_matrix(   spec,   c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0),   n_trials = 2 ) data_df <- simulate(structure, params_df, seed = 1) prepared <- prepare_data(structure, data_df) ctx <- make_context(structure) log_likelihood(ctx, prepared, list(params_df)) #> [1] 2.481128

what: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: Draw schematic view model time x-axis evidence y-axis. accumulator shown simple trajectory, timing blocking relations overlaid can inspect qualitative structure model fitting.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
code: plot_accumulators(   model,   xlim = NULL,   ylim = c(0, 1),   line_length = 1,   angle_dodge = 10,   accumulator_order = NULL,   curve_strength = 0.18,   line_col = "gray20",   line_lwd = 3,   axis_lwd = 3,   link_lwd = 2.2,   blocker_lwd = 2.6,   show_labels = TRUE,   main = "Accumulator Schematic",   xlab = "Time",   ylab = "Evidence",   ... )

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: model race model (`race_spec`, finalized model, `model_tables`). xlim Optional x-axis limits. `NULL`, chosen model. ylim Y-axis limits. line_length Length accumulator trajectory plot units. angle_dodge Target angular spacing degrees neighbouring accumulators. Angles centered 45 degrees constrained interval [10, 80]. many accumulators sharing onset, spacing reduced uniformly within onset group. accumulator_order Optional character vector accumulator labels, ordered top--bottom. affects accumulators sharing onset. curve_strength Curvature applied relationship arcs. line_col Color accumulator trajectories. line_lwd Line width accumulator trajectories. axis_lwd Line width x/y axis arrows. link_lwd Reserved reciprocal relationship arrows (currently disabled). blocker_lwd Line width blocker arrows. show_labels `TRUE`, add accumulator labels near line endpoints. main Main title. xlab X-axis label. ylab Y-axis label. ... Additional arguments passed `graphics::plot`.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
text: Invisibly returns list plotting coordinates inferred   structural links.

title: Plot accumulators and their timing relations — plot_accumulators
code: spec <- race_spec() |>   add_accumulator("go", "lognormal") |>   add_accumulator("stop", "lognormal", onset = 0.15) |>   add_pool("P", c("go", "stop")) |>   add_outcome("R", "P") |>   finalize_model() plot_accumulators(spec)

what: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
title: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
text: `prepare_data()` expands trial-level observations accumulator layout expected compiled likelihood code tags result trusted likelihood input.

title: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
code: prepare_data(structure, data_df, compress = FALSE, prep = NULL)

title: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
text: structure Finalized model structure. data_df Behavioral data. simplest case contains `trial`, `R`, `rt`; multi-outcome models can also contain `R2`, `rt2`, . compress `TRUE`, collapse repeated prepared trials attach `expand` mapping `log_likelihood()` reuse automatically. Defaults `FALSE`. prep Optional preprocessed model bundle.

title: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
text: `accumulatr_data` object.

title: Prepare behavioral data for likelihood evaluation — prepare_data
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params_df <- build_param_matrix(   spec,   c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0),   n_trials = 2 ) data_df <- simulate(structure, params_df, seed = 1) prepare_data(structure, data_df) #>   trial     R        rt accumulator onset   component #> 1     1 A_win 0.9679031           A     0 __default__ #> 2     2 A_win 0.9198333           A     0 __default__

what: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: Simulate behavioral data model

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
code: simulate(   structure,   params_df,   trial_df = NULL,   seed = NULL,   keep_detail = FALSE,   keep_component = NULL,   layout = c("auto", "rectangular", "long"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'model_structure' simulate(   structure,   params_df,   trial_df = NULL,   seed = NULL,   keep_detail = FALSE,   keep_component = NULL,   layout = c("auto", "rectangular", "long"),   ... )  # Default S3 method simulate(structure, ...)

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: structure Finalized model structure. params_df Trial-level parameter values. trial_df Optional data frame used condition simulation. includes `accumulator` column, `onset` `component` values matched trial accumulator. Otherwise, values apply trial level. seed Optional random-number seed. keep_detail `TRUE`, keep additional simulation detail. keep_component Whether keep chosen mixture component output model multiple components. `NULL`, fixed mixtures keep component label sampled mixtures drop . layout Parameter layout. `"rectangular"` expects rows ordered trial accumulator. `"long"` expects rows aligned `trial_df` inferable `component`. `"auto"` chooses automatically. ... Unused; S3 compatibility.

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
text: data frame simulated behavioral data. standard models   includes `trial`, `R`, `rt`. `n_outcomes > 1`, additional ordered   response columns `R2`/`rt2` included.

title: Simulate behavioral data from a model — simulate
code: spec <- race_spec() spec <- add_accumulator(spec, "A", "lognormal") spec <- add_outcome(spec, "A_win", "A") structure <- finalize_model(spec) params <- c(A.m = 0, A.s = 0.1, A.q = 0, A.t0 = 0) df <- build_param_matrix(spec, params, n_trials = 3) simulate(structure, df, seed = 123) #>   trial     R       rt #> 1     1 A_win 1.083347 #> 2     2 A_win 1.168675 #> 3     3 A_win 1.131959

title: AccumulatR 0.1.0
text: First public release. Added tools defining evidence-accumulation models accumulators, pools, response rules, mixture components, chained onsets. Added simulation helpers generating behavioral data model specifications. Added likelihood tools evaluating observed response-time data candidate parameter values. Added pkgdown articles reference documentation main modeling workflow.
